words.definitionEn: to protect somebody/something from attack
words.exampleEn: defend somebody/something, The role of the military is to defend the country.

words.definitionEn: a player who must stop the other team from scoring in games such as football (soccer), hockey, etc.
words.exampleEn: a passionate defender of human rights

words.definitionEn: a business or an organization that provides a particular service especially when representing other businesses or organizations
words.exampleEn: She works for an advertising agency.

words.definitionEn: a period of time when somebody/something has to wait because of a problem that makes something slow or late
words.exampleEn: Commuters will face long delays on the roads today.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody/something late or force them to do something more slowly
words.exampleEn: My flight was delayed.

words.definitionEn: a list of items to be discussed at a meeting
words.exampleEn: We have a very full agenda of issues to discuss.

words.definitionEn: to remove something that has been written or printed, or that has been stored on a computer
words.exampleEn: Your name has been deleted from the list.

words.definitionEn: done on purpose rather than by accident
words.exampleEn: a deliberate act of vandalism

words.definitionEn: done in a way that was planned, not by chance
words.exampleEn: She's been deliberately ignoring him all day.

words.definitionEn: a feeling of great pleasure
words.exampleEn: a feeling of sheer/pure delight

words.definitionEn: to give somebody a lot of pleasure and joy
words.exampleEn: This news will delight his fans all over the world.

words.definitionEn: very pleased
words.exampleEn: a delighted smile

words.definitionEn: the act of taking goods, letters, etc. to the people they have been sent to
words.exampleEn: Allow 28 days for delivery.

words.definitionEn: a very strong request for something; something that somebody needs
words.exampleEn: demand for something, a demand for higher pay

words.definitionEn: to make a very strong request for something
words.exampleEn: demand something, They are demanding the release of all political prisoners.

words.definitionEn: a system of government in which the people of a country can vote to elect their representatives
words.exampleEn: parliamentary democracy

words.definitionEn: controlled by representatives who are elected by the people of a country; connected with this system
words.exampleEn: a democratic country

words.definitionEn: to show something clearly by giving proof or evidence
words.exampleEn: demonstrate that…, New research convincingly demonstrates that age-related memory loss is not inevitable.

words.definitionEn: a public meeting or a march (= an organized walk by many people) at which people show that they are protesting against or supporting somebody/something
words.exampleEn: to take part in/go on a demonstration

words.definitionEn: to say that something is not true
words.exampleEn: deny something, to deny an allegation/a charge/an accusation

words.definitionEn: to leave a place, especially to start a trip
words.exampleEn: depart (for…) (from…), Flights for Rome depart from Terminal 3.

words.definitionEn: needing somebody/something in order to survive or be successful
words.exampleEn: a woman with several dependent children

words.definitionEn: angry, and behaving in a threatening way; ready to attack
words.exampleEn: Seals have been known to exhibit aggressive behaviour towards swimmers.

words.definitionEn: a sum of money that is given as the first part of a larger payment
words.exampleEn: They normally ask you to pay $100 (as a) deposit.

words.definitionEn: very sad and without hope
words.exampleEn: You mustn't let yourself get depressed.

words.definitionEn: making you feel very sad and without enthusiasm
words.exampleEn: a depressing sight/thought/experience

words.definitionEn: a medical condition in which a person feels very sad, anxious and without hope and often has physical symptoms such as being unable to sleep, etc.
words.exampleEn: She was diagnosed as having clinical depression.

words.definitionEn: the distance from the top or surface to the bottom of something; a particular distance down from the surface of something
words.exampleEn: depth of something, What's the depth of the water here?

words.definitionEn: to leave somebody without help or support
words.exampleEn: She was deserted by her husband.

words.definitionEn: to do something or show qualities that mean a particular reaction, reward or punishment is appropriate
words.exampleEn: deserve something, You deserve a rest after all that hard work.

words.definitionEn: a strong wish to have or do something
words.exampleEn: He now had enough money to satisfy all his desires.

words.definitionEn: to want something; to wish for something
words.exampleEn: desire something, We all desire health and happiness.

words.definitionEn: feeling or showing that you have little hope and are ready to do anything without worrying about danger to yourself or others
words.exampleEn: The prisoners grew increasingly desperate.

words.definitionEn: in a way that shows you have little hope and are ready to do anything without worrying about danger to yourself or others
words.exampleEn: She looked desperately around for a weapon.

words.definitionEn: the act of destroying something; the process of being destroyed
words.exampleEn: the destruction of the rainforests

words.definitionEn: to give a list of facts or all the available information about something
words.exampleEn: The brochure details all the hotels in the area and their facilities.

words.definitionEn: giving many details and a lot of information; paying great attention to details
words.exampleEn: a detailed description of the events

words.definitionEn: to discover or notice something, especially something that is not easy to see, hear, etc.
words.exampleEn: The tests are designed to detect the disease early.

words.definitionEn: the quality that makes you continue trying to do something even when this is difficult
words.exampleEn: fierce/grim/dogged determination

words.definitionEn: the science or practice of farming
words.exampleEn: The number of people employed in agriculture has fallen in the last decade.

words.definitionEn: to be different from somebody/something
words.exampleEn: They hold differing views.

words.definitionEn: to make a hole in the ground or to move soil from one place to another using your hands, a tool or a machine
words.exampleEn: I think I'll do some digging in the garden.

words.definitionEn: to take in a liquid, gas or other substance from the surface or space around
words.exampleEn: absorb something, Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the air.

words.definitionEn: money, food, etc. that is sent to help countries in difficult situations
words.exampleEn: humanitarian/food/medical aid

words.definitionEn: to help somebody/something to do something, especially by making it easier
words.exampleEn: aid in something, The test is designed to aid in the diagnosis of various diseases.

words.definitionEn: a physical or mental condition that makes it difficult for somebody to do some things that most other people can do
words.exampleEn: a physical/developmental/intellectual disability

words.definitionEn: having a condition that makes it difficult for you to do some things that most other people can do
words.exampleEn: physically/developmentally/intellectually disabled

words.definitionEn: a situation where people have different opinions about something and often argue
words.exampleEn: disagreement (about/on/over/as to something), Disagreement arose about exactly how to plan the show.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody feel sad because something that they hope for or expect to happen does not happen or is not as good as they hoped
words.exampleEn: disappoint (somebody), Her decision to cancel the concert is bound to disappoint her fans.

words.definitionEn: the feeling of being sad because something has not happened or been as good, successful, etc. as you expected or hoped
words.exampleEn: Book early for the show to avoid disappointment.

words.definitionEn: a thin flat round object
words.exampleEn: He wears an identity disc around his neck.

words.definitionEn: the practice of training people to obey rules and orders and punishing them if they do not; the controlled behaviour or situation that is the result of this training
words.exampleEn: The school has a reputation for high standards of discipline.

words.definitionEn: a serious illness caused by a virus called HIV that attacks the body’s ability to resist infection (the abbreviation for ‘Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome’)
words.exampleEn: the AIDS epidemic

words.definitionEn: to take an amount of money off the usual cost of something; to sell something at a discount
words.exampleEn: be discounted by something, Most of our stock has been discounted by up to 40 per cent.

words.definitionEn: to try to prevent something or to prevent somebody from doing something, especially by making it difficult to do or by showing that you do not approve of it
words.exampleEn: discourage (doing) something, a campaign to discourage smoking among teenagers

words.definitionEn: not honest; intending to trick people
words.exampleEn: Beware of dishonest traders in the tourist areas.

words.definitionEn: a thin flat round object
words.exampleEn: Red blood cells are roughly the shape of a disk.

words.definitionEn: to decide that somebody/something is not important and not worth thinking or talking about
words.exampleEn: dismiss somebody/something, to dismiss a suggestion/a claim/an idea

words.definitionEn: a condition or illness that causes problems with the way part of the body or brain works
words.exampleEn: a blood/bowel disorder

words.definitionEn: an arrangement of things in a public place to inform or entertain people or advertise something for sale
words.exampleEn: a beautiful floral display

words.definitionEn: to put something in a place where people can see it easily; to show something to people
words.exampleEn: display something, The exhibition gives local artists an opportunity to display their work.

words.definitionEn: far away in space or time
words.exampleEn: the distant sound of music

words.definitionEn: easily or clearly heard, seen, felt, etc.
words.exampleEn: There was a distinct smell of gas.

words.definitionEn: to recognize the difference between two people or things
words.exampleEn: distinguish between A and B, At what age are children able to distinguish between right and wrong?

words.definitionEn: to take somebody’s attention away from what they are trying to do
words.exampleEn: You're distracting me from my work.

words.definitionEn: to give things to a large number of people; to share something between a number of people
words.exampleEn: distribute something, The leaflets have been widely distributed.

words.definitionEn: the way that something is spread or exists over a particular area or among a particular group of people
words.exampleEn: distribution of something, They studied the geographical distribution of the disease.

words.definitionEn: any vehicle that can fly and carry goods or passengers
words.exampleEn: fighter/transport/military aircraft

words.definitionEn: an area of a country or town, especially one that has particular features
words.exampleEn: the City of London’s financial district

words.definitionEn: to interrupt somebody when they are trying to work, sleep, etc.
words.exampleEn: I'm sorry to disturb you, but can I talk to you for a moment?

words.definitionEn: a jump into deep water with your head first and your arms in front of you
words.exampleEn: a spectacular high dive (= from high above the water)

words.definitionEn: to jump into water with your head and arms going in first
words.exampleEn: dive (from/off something) (into something), We dived into the river to cool off.

words.definitionEn: very different from each other and of various kinds
words.exampleEn: People from diverse cultures were invited to the event.

words.definitionEn: a range of many people or things that are very different from each other
words.exampleEn: the biological diversity of the rainforests

words.definitionEn: a difference between two groups of people or two things that separates them from each other
words.exampleEn: He offered advice on bridging cultural divides.

words.definitionEn: the process or result of dividing into separate parts; the process or result of dividing something or sharing it out
words.exampleEn: The organism begins as a single cell and grows by cell division.

words.definitionEn: the legal ending of a marriage
words.exampleEn: The marriage ended in divorce in 1996.

words.definitionEn: to end your marriage to somebody legally
words.exampleEn: They're getting divorced.

words.definitionEn: to record something in the form of a written document, photograph, film, etc.
words.exampleEn: Urban life in the nineteenth century is well documented.

words.definitionEn: of or inside a particular country; not foreign or international
words.exampleEn: domestic affairs/politics

words.definitionEn: more important, powerful or easy to notice than other things
words.exampleEn: The firm has achieved a dominant position in the world market.

words.definitionEn: to control or have a lot of influence over somebody/something, especially in an unpleasant way
words.exampleEn: She always says a lot in meetings, but she doesn't dominate.

words.definitionEn: based on general ideas and not on any particular real person, thing or situation
words.exampleEn: abstract knowledge/principles

words.definitionEn: something that is given to a person or an organization such as a charity, in order to help them; the act of giving something in this way
words.exampleEn: a generous/large/small donation

words.definitionEn: a small round mark, especially one that is printed
words.exampleEn: There are dots above the letters i and j.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody anxious or afraid
words.exampleEn: I can only guess that they don't want to alarm the public yet.

words.definitionEn: in, towards or typical of the centre of a city, especially its main business area
words.exampleEn: a downtown store

words.definitionEn: in or towards the centre of a city, especially its main business area
words.exampleEn: to go/work downtown

words.definitionEn: the centre of a city, especially its main business area
words.exampleEn: a hotel in the heart of downtown

words.definitionEn: towards the ground; towards a lower place or position
words.exampleEn: She was lying face downwards on the grass.

words.definitionEn: a group of twelve of the same thing
words.exampleEn: Give me a dozen, please.

words.definitionEn: a group of twelve of the same thing
words.exampleEn: Give me a dozen, please.

words.definitionEn: a rough written version of something that is not yet in its final form
words.exampleEn: This is only the first draft of my speech.

words.definitionEn: to write the first rough version of something such as a letter, speech, book or law
words.exampleEn: I'll draft a letter for you.

words.definitionEn: to pull somebody/something along with effort and difficulty
words.exampleEn: drag somebody/something, The sack is too heavy to lift—you’ll have to drag it.

words.definitionEn: sudden, very great and often surprising
words.exampleEn: a dramatic increase/change/improvement/shift

words.definitionEn: very suddenly and to a very great and often surprising degree
words.exampleEn: Prices have fallen dramatically.

words.definitionEn: a long period of time when there is little or no rain
words.exampleEn: Farmers are facing ruin after two years of severe drought.

words.definitionEn: not interesting or exciting
words.exampleEn: Life in a small town could be deadly dull.

words.definitionEn: to get rid of something you do not want, especially in a place that is not suitable
words.exampleEn: Too much toxic waste is being dumped at sea.

words.definitionEn: the length of time that something lasts or continues
words.exampleEn: The school was used as a hospital for the duration of the war.

words.definitionEn: having a lot of energy and a strong personality
words.exampleEn: a dynamic leader

words.definitionEn: the study of how a society organizes its money, trade and industry
words.exampleEn: He studied politics and economics at Yale.

words.definitionEn: a person who studies or writes about economics
words.exampleEn: the World Bank's chief economist

words.definitionEn: to prepare a piece of writing, a book, etc. to be published by correcting the mistakes, making improvements to it, etc.
words.exampleEn: edit (something), This draft text will need to be edited.

words.definitionEn: the form in which a book, newspaper, etc. is published
words.exampleEn: a paperback/hardback edition

words.definitionEn: a person who is not a citizen of the country in which they live or work
words.exampleEn: an illegal alien

words.definitionEn: connected with the task of preparing something such as a newspaper, a book or a television or radio programme, to be published or broadcast
words.exampleEn: the magazine’s editorial staff

words.definitionEn: doing something in a good, careful and complete way with no waste of time, money or energy
words.exampleEn: a highly efficient worker

words.definitionEn: in a good and careful way, with no waste of time, money or energy
words.exampleEn: a very efficiently organized event

words.definitionEn: the joint between the upper and lower parts of the arm where it bends in the middle
words.exampleEn: She jabbed him with her elbow.

words.definitionEn: used as a polite word for ‘old’
words.exampleEn: an elderly man/woman/lady

words.definitionEn: to choose somebody to do a particular job by voting for them
words.exampleEn: elect somebody/something, Voters will elect a new president on 30 March next year.

words.definitionEn: the branch of science and technology that studies electric currents in electronic equipment
words.exampleEn: the electronics industry

words.definitionEn: attractive and showing a good sense of style
words.exampleEn: She was tall and elegant.

words.definitionEn: in or connected with the first stages of a course of study
words.exampleEn: an elementary English course

words.definitionEn: to remove or get rid of something
words.exampleEn: eliminate something, Credit cards eliminate the need to carry a lot of cash.

words.definitionEn: in, at or to another place or other places
words.exampleEn: Dissatisfied customers will look elsewhere.

words.definitionEn: to put your arms around somebody as a sign of love or friendship
words.exampleEn: They embraced and promised to keep in touch.

words.definitionEn: to move out of or away from something and become possible to see
words.exampleEn: The crabs emerge at low tide to look for food.

words.definitionEn: the production or sending out of light, heat, gas, etc.
words.exampleEn: the emission of carbon dioxide into the atmosphere

words.definitionEn: connected with people’s feelings (= with the emotions)
words.exampleEn: emotional problems/stress

words.definitionEn: in a way that is connected with people's feelings (= with the emotions)
words.exampleEn: emotionally disturbed children

words.definitionEn: special importance that is given to something
words.exampleEn: emphasis on/upon something, Since the elections there has been a greater emphasis on education.

words.definitionEn: to give special importance to something
words.exampleEn: emphasize something, His speech emphasized the importance of attracting industry to the town.

words.definitionEn: a group of countries or states that are controlled by one leader or government
words.exampleEn: These invasions almost led to the collapse of the Roman Empire.

words.definitionEn: to make it possible for somebody/something to do something
words.exampleEn: This new programme will enable older people to study at college.

words.definitionEn: a meeting, especially one that is sudden, unexpected or violent
words.exampleEn: a chance encounter

words.definitionEn: to experience something, especially something unpleasant or difficult, while you are trying to do something else
words.exampleEn: We encountered a number of difficulties in the first week.

words.definitionEn: to succeed in attracting and keeping somebody’s attention and interest
words.exampleEn: It is a movie that engages both the mind and the eye.

words.definitionEn: to increase or further improve the good quality, value or status of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: This is an opportunity to enhance the reputation of the company.

words.definitionEn: giving pleasure
words.exampleEn: an enjoyable weekend/experience

words.definitionEn: an official process to find out the cause of something or to find out information about something
words.exampleEn: a murder enquiry
